title: Translanguaging as Scaffolding in Chinese Writing Classrooms: Perceptions and Practices of Non‐Cantonese‐Speaking Students in Hong Kong Secondary Schools
author: Karen Cheung Ching Ching, Yang Ruowei
abstract: ABSTRACT Translanguaging has been empirically proven to have constructive and even transformative effects, as it provides linguistic minority learners with access to various educational and linguistic resources. Nevertheless, there is limited research on its use in the context of Chinese writing as a second language and, more specifically, on how it supports the co‐construction of knowledge betwee…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, and MICM"在学科领域（CSL教学）和理论资源（translanguaging作为支架）上有交集，但其核心关注的是教师-学生间的知识共建以及学生感知，而非同伴互动中的meaning negotiation或LRE微观机制，因此属于"部分相关——共享教学语境但研究焦点不同"的层级。

title: Translingual practice as a discursive strategy to shape lifestyle and cultural identity
author: Yunhong Wang, Boyang Peng, Xiaoli Tang
abstract: The paper presents a comprehensive examination of the influence of online translingual practice on the lifestyle choices and identity construction of Chinese youth. An analysis of data sourced from platforms such as Weibo and Xiaohongshu finds that the emergence of new lifestyles facilitated by translingual practice transcends traditional social norms, leading to notable shifts in lifestyle prefer…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"高度对齐：它同样聚焦小红书等中国社交平台上的跨语言实践，并将其与身份建构和生活方式话语直接关联，构成你Beyond Deficit稿件最直接的对话文献之一。

title: Learning Mandarin-as-a-Foreign Language: Communities of Practice and Translanguaging
author: Yajie Zhang
abstract: Mandarin-as-a-foreign-language (MFL) courses are popular in English-medium international schools in China where international students are often bilingual or multilingual. These students may choose to communicate in more than one language. This research investigates teachers’ and students’ perceptions of language choices while teaching and learning MFL at an English-medium i…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在场景层高度重叠——同样聚焦中国EMI环境下的translanguaging实践，且涉及多语学生的语言选择与课堂社群构建，可以直接作为你稿件的文献对话对象，帮助你在literature review中精确定位自己研究的差异贡献（如：该文侧重师生感知/Communities of Practice框架，而你的稿件聚焦身份协商与教师行动研究的变革性维度）。最值得借鉴的是问题意识的边界设定方式：它将translanguaging限定在"MFL课堂中的language choices及师生对此的perceptions"，问题清晰但偏描述性，恰好可以反衬你的稿件如何把问题写得更"尖锐"——从描述"是否/如何translanguage"推进到"translanguaging如何成为身份协商的变革工具"，从而避免你的论证停留在经验总结层面。

title: Teacher Agency Through Translanguaging in English-Medium Community Schools
author: Nani Babu Ghimire, Yam Prasad Pandeya, Bhanu Bhakta Gurung
abstract: The development of teacher agency in teaching academic subjects in English within community schools has become a critical global issue, including in Nepal. This study examines how teachers create agency through translanguaging practices while implementing the English Medium Instruction (EMI) policy in Nepal’s multilingual classroom context. The study employed an ethnographic research design within…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"直接相关，尤其在"教师能动性（teacher agency）与translanguaging在EMI政策下的互动关系"这一层面与你的稿件共享核心关切——你的稿件聚焦EMI语法课堂中教师通过行动研究推动translanguaging实践，而这篇则从民族志视角考察教师如何在政策压力下自发生成translanguaging agency，两者构成"自上而下的行动研究干预"与"自下而上的教师自发策略"之间的对话。

title: Exploring Emerging Translanguaging Practices among International Undergraduates in EAP Courses in South Korea
author: Jinsil Jang
abstract: As the number of international students in South Korean universities increases, top-tiered institutions are gradually adopting English-Medium Instruction (EMI) policies without considering the linguistic diversity of these students (Lee & Lee, 2018). Similarly, my English for Academic Purposes (EAP) courses were initially designed to be solely conducted in English, assuming that it would be th…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"高度吻合：同样聚焦EMI语境下自然涌现的translanguaging实践，同样以教师-研究者视角反思英语唯一政策的局限，且研究场景（韩国大学EAP课堂中的国际学生）与你的中国EMI语法课堂形成可直接对话的平行案例。

title: Exploring Language Education: Global and Local Perspectives
author: Bardel, Camilla 1963-, Christina Hedman, Katarina Rejman et al.
abstract: The overarching aim of this book is to offer researchers and students insight into some currently discussed issues at the Swedish as well as the international research frontline of Language Education in a selection of up-to-date work. Another aim is to provide teachers, teacher educators and policy-makers with input from research within the interconnected disciplines of Applied Linguistics, Langua…
relevance: 这篇论文是一本以瑞典及国际语言教育前沿议题为主题的编著，涵盖应用语言学、语言教育与二语习得的交叉领域，与你的研究画像在宏观学科归属上有重叠，但摘要显示其内容为多主题概览性质（"a selection of up-to-date work"），并未聚焦于CSL同伴互动、意义协商、LRE或MICM中的任何一条，与你今日主线仅属学科层面的远距离相关。

title: Peer Interaction in Chinese as a Second Language: The Interplay of NoM, LREs, and Translanguaging
author: Shen, Jiaxin
abstract: While much research has been conducted on interaction in second language acquisition, the interplay of key interactional mechanisms—Negotiation of Meaning (NoM), Language-Related Episodes (LREs), and translanguaging—remains underexplored in the context of Chinese as a Second Language (CSL). With Mandarin gaining prominence globally, understanding how CSL learners use these strategies during peer i…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, and MICM"高度重合，几乎是同一研究空间内的直接对话对象——它同样考察NoM、LREs与translanguaging在CSL同伴互动中的共现与交互，因此你需要明确你的MICM稿件与该研究在理论框架（是否提出可检验的机制模型而非仅描述共现）和分析粒度上的区分点。

title: Assessment for learning or assessment for scoring? Washback of analytic rubrics in Vietnamese EFL classrooms
author: Hoang Yen Phuong, Lê Thanh Thảo, Trut Thuy Pham
abstract: This study investigates the washback effects of EFL lecturers’ use of analytic rubrics for assessing students’ writing performance in Vietnamese higher education. Although analytic rubrics are widely promoted for enhancing assessment transparency and supporting student learning, little is known about how lecturers implement them in practice and how their use influences teaching and learning behavi…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, and epistemic authority"在制度化评估工具如何在实践中偏离其设计意图这一层面上直接相关：它考察analytic rubrics从"促进学习"滑向"服务评分"的washback机制，与你对AP Chinese评分标准中元语言不对称如何重新分配认识权威的论证共享同一个核心张力——评估文件的制度设计逻辑与课堂实际效果之间的落差。

title: Standardized proficiency tests in a campus-wide English curriculum: a washback study
author: Shao‐Ting Alan Hung, Heng‐Tsung Danny Huang
abstract: Washback refers to the influence of tests on learning and teaching. To date, a number of studies have revealed that tests affect teaching content, course design, and classroom practices. However, in Asian higher education contexts, little research has examined the washback of proficiency tests on English learning in comparison with the efforts on teaching. Thus, the current study bridged this rese…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, and epistemic authority"在评估反拨效应（washback）这一层面上存在方法与问题意识的交集：两者都关注标准化考试如何通过制度设计反向塑造教与学的实践，但该文聚焦的是高等教育英语水平测试对学生学习行为的影响，与你所做的AP中文文件层面的批判性话语分析在理论深度和分析对象上差异明显——它更偏向经验性washback研究，缺少你所需要的认识论权威（epistemic authority）或元语言不对称视角。

title: From tests to tasks: how Vietnamese EFL teachers navigate washback through formative assessment practices
author: Hoang Yen Phuong, Ngoc Bao Chau Tran, Thị Thùy Linh Nguyễn et al.
abstract: In Vietnamese English language classrooms, assessment has long been shaped by the weight of summative exams and the pressures of test-oriented instruction. Yet in the margins of this exam-driven culture, some teachers are beginning to reimagine assessment, not as a final judgment, but as a process embedded in learning itself. This study explores how Vietnamese EFL teachers respond to washback by a…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, epistemic authority"的关联处于制度-washback层面的平行对话，而非直接对接。它同样关注高风险考试体制如何形塑课堂评估实践，且聚焦教师在制度压力下的能动性（从summative到formative的转向），这与你在LAQ稿件中论证AP Chinese考试文件如何通过评分标准分配认识权威、压缩元语言多样性的逻辑形成镜像：它看的是教师如何在washback中"挪用"空间，你看的是制度文件本身如何预设并封闭这一空间。

title: English-medium instruction in Tunisia: Perspectives of students
author: Marii Abdeljaoued
abstract: This article gives a Tunisian perspective to the ongoing debate on the adoption of English-medium instruction (EMI) across the globe and notably in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA). It explores the attitudes of students toward EMI, especially in relation to French, the default medium of instruction at Tunisian universities. It also investigates the challenges that students encounter in cour…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms）在"EMI课堂实践"这一层面上有表面交集，但实际关联较浅：它聚焦的是学生对EMI的态度与挑战（perception study），而非translanguaging作为教学转化策略或身份协商过程，也不涉及行动研究方法论，因此与你稿件的理论内核和方法设计距离较远。

title: The Role of the L1 in EMI classroom practices
author: Balbina Moncada-Comas
journal: CLIL Journal of Innovation and Research in Plurilingual and Pluricultural Education
abstract: English-medium instruction (EMI) affects lecturers’ classroom practices as they face a new teaching scenario where language(s) can be used to construct lecturers’ identities. Therefore, lecturers can take up different identities because their language-choice acts depend on their communicative and identification purposes. Employing Membership-Categorisation Analysis (MCA) to examine classroom inter…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"高度贴合：它同样聚焦EMI课堂中语言选择与教师身份建构的关系，且以真实课堂互动数据为分析对象，直接对话你稿件中"translanguaging如何介入身份协商"这一核心论点。

title: Competence beyond language: translanguaging and spatial repertoire in teacher-student interaction in a music classroom in an international Chinese University
author: Amy Wanyu Ou, Mingyue Gu
journal: International Journal of Bilingual Education and Bilingualism
abstract: Previous research on classroom interaction in international university contexts tended to focus on individual speakers’ language (in)competence. This paper adopts a translanguaging and spatial orientation to intercultural classroom interaction and highlights the role of situated assemblages of linguistic, semiotic and multimodal resources embedded in the environment of learning activities for succ…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论视角上直接相关，但侧重点有所偏移：它将translanguaging从语言能力框架拓展到spatial repertoire与多模态符号资源的组装，关注的是课堂沟通如何超越个体语言能力，而非身份协商或教师行动研究。对你的EMI稿件而言，它最值得借鉴的是问题意识的锐化方式——论文开篇即以"competence beyond language"挑战既有研究将课堂互动窄化为个体语言（不）能力的倾向，这种"精准否定已有文献默认前提"的写法，正好回应你"把研究问题写得尖锐但不过度夸张"的长期目标。你可以参考它如何用一个清晰的对立面（individual language competence vs. situated assemblage）来定位自己的贡献，同时将多模态会话分析作为evidence chain呈现发现，而非停留在经验描述。

title: “Our country has gained independence, but we haven't”: Collaborative translanguaging to decolonize English language teaching
author: Shakina Rajendram
abstract: Abstract The colonial history of many English language teaching (ELT) contexts has shaped how the concept of language is understood, how language policies are constructed, and how language education is organized. Various aspects of ELT in countries that were colonized continue to promote the imperialism of English (Motha, 2014) through the naming (i.e., labeling of linguistic phenomena as distinct…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接对接度有限——它聚焦后殖民ELT情境下的translanguaging实践与去殖民化议程，而非课堂互动中的意义协商机制或LRE编码。但它与你活跃稿件中"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"以及"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"所涉及的语言意识形态批判线更为贴近，尤其是其对语言"命名、分离与等级化"（naming, separation, hierarchization）这一分析框架的提出，可以为你在DLI或EMI稿件中讨论translanguaging如何挑战单语规范提供一个简洁的批判性透镜。

title: Commonalities and Conflation of Global Englishes and Translanguaging for Equitable English Language Education
author: Fan Fang, Yudie Xu
journal: Teaching English as a Second or Foreign Language--TESL-EJ
abstract: This conceptual paper aims to review some commonalities between two paradigms: Global Englishes and translanguaging. It does so by considering the postcolonial varieties of English, the challenge of native speakerism ideology and the inclusion of multiple discursive practices in classroom discourse. This paper argues that both paradigms should be recognised and incorporated into current English la…
relevance: 这篇概念性论文讨论Global Englishes与translanguaging两个范式的共性与混淆，核心关切在于英语教育中的语言意识形态与公平性，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, MICM）之间没有直接的方法或数据层关联，最多在宏观理论层面——translanguaging作为课堂话语资源——与你MICM稿件中可能涉及的translanguaging视角有边缘交集。

title: Translanguaging as a decolonising approach: students’ perspectives towards integrating Indigenous epistemology in language teaching
author: Danping Wang
abstract: Abstract This study explores how translanguaging has been enacted in a university-wide curriculum transformation project in an additional language programme in Aotearoa New Zealand. Its aim is to reveal students’ perspectives on integrating Indigenous epistemology into the curriculum of a beginner-level Chinese course. The survey data, collected from 155 students, show that most students react pos…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"在主题词上高度重叠（translanguaging、数字多模态写作、中文教学语境），但其核心关切是将translanguaging作为去殖民化课程改革的工具，聚焦的是大学课程设计中Indigenous epistemology的整合与学生态度，与你稿件关注的平台自发话语实践、功能不对称分类及身份协商机制属于不同层面的问题。

title: Making sense of trans-translating in blogger subtitling: a netnographic approach to translanguaging on a Chinese microblogging site
author: Yifei Mao, Zhong Lin, Feng Wang
abstract: Abstract The online digital sphere has recently witnessed a rapid growing of amateur audiovisual translation, which not only reinvigorates the current body of translation scholarship but also calls for a paradigm shift from the entrenched monolingualism. Though a plethora of studies have been conducted on fansubbing, little is known about blogger subtitling. Drawing on the netnographic data from a…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"高度对齐：它同样聚焦中国社交平台（微博）上中英语言资源的混合使用，同样采用网络民族志方法考察用户生成内容中的translanguaging实践，且关注平台特有的多模态形式（字幕翻译视频）。这为你的小红书研究提供了一个直接的对话对象——你可以在文献综述中将其定位为"已有研究关注翻译类博主的translanguaging，但尚未考察非翻译语境下普通用户如何策略性地部署双语资源完成身份建构与社群参与"，从而更精准地刻画你的研究缺口。

title: Chinese young people’s attitudes towards translanguaging in self-praise on social media
author: Wei Ren, Yaping Guo, Li Wei
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"，因为它同样聚焦中国年轻人在社交媒体上的中英转换实践，并将其与身份建构（自我呈现/自我赞扬）联系起来，与你Xiaohongshu稿件在研究场景、人群和核心现象上高度重叠，可以作为文献综述中直接对话的近邻研究。

title: Exploring conceptual understanding in teacher–student mediation in a Spanish as a foreign language classroom in China: A translanguaging perspective
author: Danli Li, Ge Xu, Jian Qin
abstract: Previous research on translanguaging in classrooms for a language other than English (LOTE) has shown that it helps learners compensate for limited language proficiency. Few studies have investigated how translanguaging promotes learners’ conceptual understanding of the target language. This study aims to investigate how a Chinese teacher mediates conceptual understanding of the target language am…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"高度相关，但侧重点不同：它聚焦translanguaging如何促进目标语概念理解（conceptual understanding），而非身份协商，且语境为LOTE（西班牙语）课堂而非EMI课堂。不过，它同样处理教师主导的pedagogical translanguaging在中国大学课堂中的微观运作，与你的稿件共享"教师中介"这一分析视角。

title: Translingual practice in remote EFL tertiary education: How multilingual speakers create translanguaging spaces
author: Akhmad Hairul Umam, Setiono Sugiharto, Christine Manara
abstract: Published studies on translingual practice in the pedagogical realms have burgeoned in the current literature, generating important insights into how communication has become dynamic and fluid. However, these studies have focused almost exclusively on face-to-face, in-person interactions. As COVID-19, which hit all domains of life (including education) worldwide, has compelled schools to conduct r…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论概念层相关，但属于部分相关而非核心对标：它聚焦远程EFL课堂中多语使用者如何创建translanguaging空间，与你的EMI语法课堂行动研究在场景（远程vs.面授）、学科定位（EFL vs. EMI）和方法论取向（观察描述vs.行动研究）上都有距离，且未涉及身份协商或教师行动研究维度。

title: The translanguaging practices of expert Indian teachers of English and their learners
author: Jason Anderson
journal: Journal of Multilingual and Multicultural Development
abstract: This paper reports on a comparative case study of the multilingual practices of eight secondary teachers of English from across India, all identified as experts of their contexts using multiple criteria. Both qualitative and quantitative data from classroom observations, interviews and other sources were collected, analysed and compared across cases. It finds that, while there was noticeable varia…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction / MICM"的直接对接度有限——它聚焦的是教师端的translanguaging实践而非学习者之间的同伴互动或意义协商机制，且研究对象是印度英语课堂而非CSL情境。不过，它与你活跃稿件中"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"更为贴近，尤其是"教师如何在多语课堂中将学习者的语言资源纳入互动以优先保障参与"这一发现，可以为你讨论translanguaging作为教学行动的机制性主张提供比较视角。

title: Observing a teacher’s interactional competence in an ESOL classroom: a translanguaging perspective
author: Kevin W. H. Tai, David Wei Dai
abstract: Abstract Research on translanguaging practices in multilingual contexts has explored how translanguaging highlights the multilingual and multicultural nature of social interactions and its transformative nature in transgressing established norms and boundaries. This article aims to provide an alternative view of interactional competence by connecting it to the notion of translanguaging and its emp…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction / MICM"的关联处于理论-概念层而非直接的方法或数据层：它将translanguaging视角嫁接到interactional competence (IC)上，试图重新定义IC的边界，这与你在MICM稿件中需要把"多语互动能力"这一机制性主张写得既有理论锚点又可操作化的需求直接对话——你可以借鉴（或明确区分）它对IC的扩展定义，来为你自己的MICM模型划定概念边界。

title: Global Influence and Local Adaptation: The Integration of English in Chinese Internet Slang
author: Bartlomiej Kuszel
journal: International Journal of Education and Humanities
abstract: This study explores the influence of English on Chinese internet slang, focusing on how English-derived acronyms, morphemes, and code-switching have become integrated into online Chinese discourse. Although China is typically categorized as a low-proficiency English environment, many elements originating from English continue to enter Chinese social media, creating hybrid forms. This study aims to…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"在平台语境和语言混合现象层面存在表面交集（都关注中英混用在中国社交平台上的表现），但其核心问题意识停留在接触语言学/词汇借用层面（英语元素如何被本土化吸收），与你稿件所关切的translanguaging作为身份建构资源、功能不对称性以及"第三空间"中的权力协商有本质区别。

title: Young chinese immigrant children’s language and literacy practices on social media: a translanguaging perspective
author: Sumin Zhao, Rosie Flewitt
abstract: In this paper, we present a research approach that makes visible how young children in Chinese immigrant families muster their multilingual, multimodal and multisemiotic repertoires as they interact with distant family and friends on social media. The approach brings multimodal social semiotics into conversation with translanguaging to problematize the notion of languages as bounded systems, and t…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"高度契合：同样聚焦华人移民群体在社交媒体上的跨语言实践，同样将多模态社会符号学与translanguaging理论对接，且同样关注数字平台如何成为超越课堂的语言实践空间。它为你的Xiaohongshu稿件提供了一个直接的"对话对象"——你可以在文献定位中指出，已有研究（如本文）主要考察儿童/家庭层面的数字跨语言实践，而你的研究则将视角推向成人学习者社区中translanguaging的不对称功能分类与身份建构，从而锐化你的贡献声明。

title: TikTok Refugees, Digital Migration, and the Expanding Affordances of Xiaohongshu (RedNote) for Informal Language Learning
abstract: The mass influx of English-speaking "TikTok refugees" to Xiaohongshu (also known as RedNote) has not only reshaped Xiaohongshu into a dynamic space for intercultural exchange but has also unlocked a wealth of opportunities for informal language learning within an integrated, accessible, and engaging translingual and transcultural digital ecosystem.Building upon the theoretical construct of languag…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"高度重叠：它同样聚焦小红书平台上的跨语言实践，且以"TikTok refugees"数字迁移为语境讨论平台affordances如何促成非正式语言学习，这与你Beyond Deficit稿件共享平台场景和translingual生态的核心设定，但它侧重的是language learning affordances（van Lier, 2004），而非你关注的身份建构与权力不对称功能分类，因此理论切口和论证目标不同。

title: Teaching English in an engineering international branch campus: a collaborative autoethnography of our emotion labor
author: Sara Hillman, Aymen Elsheikh, Naqaa Abbas et al.
journal: IRAL - International Review of Applied Linguistics in Language Teaching
abstract: Abstract While a number of studies have documented the significant role of emotions and the emotion labor produced in English language teaching, research exploring English instructors’ emotion labor in transnational higher education contexts such as international branch campuses (IBCs) and within Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) programs is lacking. Arguably, these neoliberally-dr…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, and institutional discourse"高度贴合——虽然语境不同（IBC工科院校 vs. 双语沉浸项目），但核心关切一致：教师在新自由主义制度话语中被结构性边缘化所产生的情感劳动与脆弱性，且同样涉及制度权力如何通过学科等级、语言地位差异转化为教师个体的情感负担。

title: Understanding the Emotional Labor of English Language Teaching while Black in the United States
author: Malik Stevenson
abstract: ABSTRACT There is a robust literature examining emotional labor as it is experienced by English language teachers and how it is prompted by the emotional rules of their employers. However, it has not been sufficiently inclusive of native English‐speaking Black English language teachers (BELTs). Using the method of autoethnography I examine my experiences with emotional labor as I struggle to manag…
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合今日主线"Teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, and institutional discourse"的核心关切——教师在制度性情感规则（emotional rules）下的脆弱性与身份挣扎，只是情境从双语沉浸转向了IEP中的种族化体验。它对你当前草稿最有借鉴价值的不是理论框架本身（emotional labor已是成熟概念），而是如何把高度个人化的自传民族志经历转化为制度层面的结构性分析——即作者怎样让"个人故事"服务于对institutional emotional rules这一权力机制的批判，而非停留在叙事共鸣层面，这恰好对应你今天最需要强化的写作能力。具体而言，值得关注作者在编码与证据呈现上如何将个人情感事件（race-related stress）系统性地连接到雇主/机构的情感规范，从而使"vulnerability"成为一个可分析的结构位置而非仅仅是一种感受描述。

title: Emotionally (in)hospitable spaces: reflecting on language teacher–teacher educator collaboration as a source of emotion labor and emotional capital
author: Carlo Cinaglia, D. Philip Montgomery, Matthew D. Coss
journal: IRAL - International Review of Applied Linguistics in Language Teaching
abstract: Abstract While various aspects of language teachers’ emotional experiences have been gaining attention, including emotion labor and emotional capital, less attention has been placed on the emotional experiences of teacher educators supporting language teachers in emotionally challenging situations. Following calls to examine language teachers’ emotional experiences ecologically and as socially and…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, and institutional discourse"高度契合：它直接处理语言教师与教师教育者在制度性情感压力下的合作关系，将emotion labor和emotional capital置于生态化、制度化框架中分析，这与你稿件中教师脆弱性如何被制度话语塑造的核心关切几乎同构。

title: Lecturers’ translanguaging practices in English-taught lectures in Turkey
author: Eda Genc, Doğan Yüksel, Samantha Curle
journal: Journal of Multilingual Theories and Practices
abstract: This article presents a qualitative study investigating lecturers’ translanguaging practices in English medium instruction (EMI) courses in a Turkish higher education setting. Specifically, a comprehensive investigation of the functions of translanguaging used by lecturers was conducted following Lo’s and Sahan and Rose’s frameworks, which propose pedagogical and social and affective functions. Ei…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接关联度较低——它聚焦的是教师端在EMI课堂中的translanguaging功能分类，而非学习者同伴互动中的意义协商或语言相关片段（LRE）。不过，它与你的活跃稿件"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"高度对口，也可间接为MICM稿件中讨论translanguaging如何在课堂互动中实现内容传递提供功能分类的参照框架。

title: Translanguaging as a Mediational Tool in the Process of Feedback in Second Language Writing
author: Danli Li, Yibei Wang
abstract: Abstract A growing number of studies have focused on pedagogical translanguaging, helping to develop students’ writing competence in second language (L2) classrooms. However, the role of translanguaging in the feedback from both teacher and students in L2 writing remains scant. Adopting a sociocultural approach, this study aims to investigate how translanguaging practices in L2 writing tutorials c…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, MICM）在理论层和分析单位上有直接交集：它同样采用社会文化理论视角考察互动中的translanguaging如何中介L2写作学习，其"反馈过程中的translanguaging作为中介工具"这一机制性主张，与你MICM稿件中试图提出的互动机制模型在逻辑结构上高度平行——都需要在"描述现象"与"提出可检验的中介机制"之间找到平衡。

title: Agency and Register in Translanguaging: Middle School Bilingual Learners Engaging in Social Studies Inquiry
author: Mary J. Schleppegrell, Mina Hernández García, Sally AL‐Banna et al.
abstract: Abstract We report on U.S. middle school Arabic‐speaking students who were agentive in translanguaging for disciplinary learning during social studies inquiry and suggest specific ways that students' academic learning and bilingual development can be supported through translanguaging in the context of English‐dominant classrooms. We engaged in design‐based research with teachers and students over …
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction / MICM"在方法层和理论框架层有中等相关性，但学科语境（社会学科探究中的translanguaging）与你的CSL课堂互动/meaning negotiation焦点并不完全重叠。它最贴近的是你活跃稿件中"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"以及"MICM"稿件中关于学习者能动性（agency）如何驱动互动机制的那条线——尤其是学生在同伴互动中如何调动多语资源完成学科任务，这与你MICM模型中peer interaction的能动性维度可以形成对话。

title: Linguistic Identities in the Digital Space
author: Enikő Biró
abstract: Abstract Within the digital world, new multilingual contacts appeared, which led to a more multilingual Web and enabled local and global participation that assert new identities (Lenihan and Kelly-Holmes 2017). For multilingual people, language choice and code-switching serves as a means for users to perform a specific image of cultural or personal identity and signal their affiliation with a part…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（Xiaohongshu上的中英translanguaging与身份建构）在主题层面直接相关——都关注数字空间中双语/多语使用者如何通过语言选择进行身份展演与社群归属标记，且同样以社交媒体公开数据为语料。

title: Exploring the Bilingual Use of Chinese and English Among Chinese Students in the UK on the Chinese Social Media Platform Xiaohongshu
author: Zhihan Liu
abstract: This study explores the phenomenon of bilingualism among Chinese international students in the United Kingdom and its representation on Xiaohongshu, a popular Chinese social media platform. With the increasing trend of Chinese students studying abroad, bilingualism has become a significant aspect of their identity. This paper examines how these students use both Chinese and English on Xiaohongshu,…
relevance: 这篇论文与你的"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"稿件在平台语境和研究对象上高度重叠——同样关注在英中国留学生在小红书上的中英双语使用与身份建构，构成直接的对话文献。然而，从摘要来看，该研究将现象框定为"bilingualism"而非translanguaging，且以"dual identities and cultural adaptation"这类较传统的二元框架切入，恰好为你的稿件提供了一个可以明确"超越"的理论对照点：你可以在贡献陈述中精准指出，既有小红书双语研究仍停留在语码并置的描述层面，而你的功能分类与不对称translanguaging框架揭示了权力与身份协商的更深层机制。最值得借鉴的不是它的方法或理论，而是它作为问题意识的反面参照——帮助你在introduction和contribution段落中把"为什么translanguaging视角比bilingualism描述更有解释力""为什么功能分类而非简单频次统计才能回应deficit话语"这两个论证锚点写得更尖锐、更有文献支撑。

title: A Sociolinguistic Examination of Multilingual Phenomena in Virtual Network Environments
author: Xuanqi Cao
abstract: Against globalization and rapid advancements in information technology, social media has become a vital platform for multilingual users to construct identity and engage in cross-cultural communication. Adopting a sociolinguistic perspective, this study selects the Instagram comment section of a popular Chinese-American comedian as its research site, aiming to analyze the linguistic practices and s…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（Xiaohongshu上的中英translanguaging与身份建构）在研究场景和大方向上高度重叠——都聚焦社交媒体评论区的中英多语实践与身份工作，但从摘要判断，它停留在传统社会语言学的code-mixing分类层面，缺乏translanguaging理论的批判深度，也未见系统的多模态分析或平台可供性讨论，因此理论与方法层面对你稿件的直接借鉴价值有限。

title: Tensions in an Identity‐Oriented Language Teaching Practicum: A Dialogic Approach
author: Özgehan Uştuk, Bedrettin Yazan
abstract: Abstract In this study, we examine how teacher candidates navigate the tensions in their identity work as they complete the activities in the TESOL practicum course in Turkiye. Using the Bakhtinian approach to teacher identity, we conceptually maintain that identity work inevitably involves tensions that teachers encounter during their professional lives. We use a qualitative case study design to …
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日主线"teacher identity, institutional discourse, and emotion"中的教师身份与制度话语维度——它聚焦实习教师在TESOL课程制度框架下经历的身份张力，且以Bakhtin对话理论将个人叙事升格为结构性分析（dialogic tension不只是个人感受，而是制度声音与个人声音的碰撞），这与你"Teacher Vulnerability / DLI"稿件中教师在制度处境中的身份协商高度同构。

title: Mathematics identity research: the state of the art and future directions
author: Mellony Graven, Einat Heyd‐Metzuyanim
abstract: Identity research in mathematics education has become increasingly prominent over the past two decades. In the last few years, there have been several reviews of identity literature in the field of mathematics education generally, or specifically focused on mathematics learner identities or mathematics teacher identity. We begin our paper by summarizing the key findings of these reviews, pointing …
relevance: 这篇论文是数学教育领域的身份研究综述，与你今日主线"teacher identity, institutional discourse"在身份理论层面有交叉，但学科语境（数学教育）和核心关切（数学学习者/教师的学科身份建构）与你聚焦的语言教育、制度话语及情感政治有明显距离，属于跨学科方法层的部分相关。

title: Emotion AI at Work: Implications for Workplace Surveillance, Emotional Labor, and Emotional Privacy
author: Kat Roemmich, Florian Schaub, Nazanin Andalibi
abstract: Workplaces are increasingly adopting emotion AI, promising benefits to organizations. However, little is known about the perceptions and experiences of workers subject to emotion AI in the workplace. Our interview study with (n=15) US adult workers addresses this gap, finding that (1) participants viewed emotion AI as a deep privacy violation over the privacy of workers’ sensitive emotional inform…
relevance: 这篇论文与你"Teacher Vulnerability / DLI"稿件及今日主线中"institutional discourse and emotion"最为贴近：它将职场情感AI监控解读为制度性权力对个体情感隐私的侵入，这与你关注的教师在制度处境中如何经历情感脆弱性（vulnerability）、被迫进行情感劳动（emotional labor）的问题意识高度同构——只是场景从学校换到了更广义的工作场所。

title: Society in digital contexts: New modes of identity and community construction
author: Najma Al Zidjaly
abstract: This thematic volume of sociolinguistic research focuses on identity and community construction at the interface of the local and the global, examining these phenomena using varied research methods and through the lenses of diverse analytical and theoretical perspectives. The papers highlight the intricacies inherent in identity and community construction in digitized contexts through the explorat…
relevance: 这篇论文作为一部聚焦数字语境中身份与社区建构的社会语言学专题集，与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"在研究场域和核心关切上直接对齐——它同样关注数字平台上语言/符号资源如何被调用来完成身份工作，且强调本地与全球交汇处的动态张力，这正是你Xiaohongshu稿件需要论证的平台语境特殊性（digital third space作为跨国、跨语言身份协商场所）。

title: Doing expertise multilingually and multimodally in online English teaching videos
author: Wing Yee Jenifer Ho, Kevin W. H. Tai
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"高度契合：它同样聚焦数字平台上多语多模态实践如何建构特定身份（此处为"专家身份"），与你Xiaohongshu稿件中translanguaging的身份功能论证形成直接对话——尤其是你需要论证translanguaging在平台语境中不仅是语言混用，而是完成特定社会行动（identity work）的资源。

title: From Rural China to the Digital Wilds: Negotiating Digital Repertoires to Claim the Right to Speak
author: Guangxiang Liu, Ron Darvin
abstract: Abstract Based on data from a qualitative case study of two Chinese university EFL learners from rural backgrounds, Andy and Jimmy, this study traces their progress from being struggling English language learners to confident speakers of English. Drawing on Darvin and Norton's (2015) model of investment that recognizes the intersection of identity, capital, and ideology, this study dissects Andy a…
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"：它同样聚焦中国学习者在非正式数字空间中的语言实践与身份协商，且以Darvin & Norton的investment模型将数字repertoires、资本与意识形态交叉分析，与你Beyond Deficit稿件中对小红书上translanguaging功能分类及身份论证的理论关切高度重叠。

title: Implementing a formative assessment model at the secondary schools: attitudes and challenges
author: Kh. Atikur Rahman, Md. Kamrul Hasan, Ehsan Namaziandost et al.
abstract: Abstract This paper aims at exploring the issues related to implementing a formative assessment model namely School-Based Assessment (SBA) at secondary schools in Bangladesh. A qualitative study employing interviews with English teachers of grade 8 and the head teachers and FGDs with students from twelve secondary schools was conducted. Moreover, field notes and document analysis provided valuable…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, epistemic authority"的关联度较低，仅在最宽泛的"评估政策落地困境"层面有交集：它关注的是孟加拉国中学校本形成性评估（SBA）的实施障碍，属于教育评估政策的执行研究，而你的核心关切是标准化考试文件如何通过元语言框架分配认识论权威——两者的分析对象、理论层级和批判深度差异显著。

title: The language assessment literacy needs of Iranian EFL teachers with a focus on reformed assessment policies
author: Tahereh Firoozi, Kioumars Razavipour, Alireza Ahmadi
abstract: Teachers’ assessment literacy has recently captured the attention of scholars across various educational contexts. The literature has it that there is a gap between teachers’ assessment practices and national assessment policies. The present study investigated the assessment needs of Iranian EFL teachers in the wake of the new assessment reform, which aims at replacing traditional discrete point t…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, epistemic authority"在制度性评估政策与教师实践之间的落差这一层高度相关——二者都在追问：当国家级评估文件推行某种测试理念时，一线教师的理解与实践如何被制度性地错位或边缘化。你的AP Chinese稿件聚焦的是评估框架本身如何通过元语言设计分配认识论权威，而这篇文章从教师assessment literacy需求端切入，恰好构成一个镜像视角，可以帮你在limitations或discussion中界定你的分析边界——即你的文件批判并不等同于教师实际解读的研究，二者需要区分。

title: Exploring the affordances of multimodal competence, multichannel awareness and plurilingual lecturing in EMI
author: Maria Sabaté Dalmau, Balbina Moncada-Comas
abstract: In this paper we expand on a theoretical-methodological framework for micro sociolinguistic multimodal analysis of situated practices in English-Medium Instruction (EMI) research (Moncada-Comas, & Sabaté-Dalmau, in press). We examine an interaction of a lecturer and his 21 students in an engineering class at a Catalan university. Drawing from video/audio-recorded classroom interactions, interviews…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在EMI课堂微观互动分析层面直接相关：它同样关注EMI教师如何在实际授课中调动超越单一英语的资源（多模态能力、多语实践），这与你稿件中translanguaging作为课堂转化实践的核心论点形成理论对话——尤其是它将translanguaging扩展到多模态与多渠道维度，可以帮助你在讨论部分定位自己研究的理论边界（你聚焦identity negotiation，而非multimodal competence）。

title: CLIL teacher online professional development in translanguaging and trans-semiotizing: a pedagogy of multiliteracies
author: Mercedes Querol-Julián
journal: Journal of Multilingual and Multicultural Development
abstract: Theories of translanguaging and trans-semiotizing have gained relevance in the design of learning in CLIL classrooms.However, while many studies have shown the benefits of these pedagogies, scarce research has explored CLIL teacher professional development (PD) in this field.In this paper, I intend to measure the effectiveness of an online pedagogy of multiliteracies, which embraces translanguagin…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论资源上有交集（translanguaging + 课堂教学设计），但其核心关注点是教师职业发展项目的效果测量，而非课堂内身份协商或行动研究方法论，因此属于"部分相关——共享理论术语但研究问题层不同"的关系。

title: Creative translanguaging in formative assessment: Chinese teachers’ perceptions and practices in the primary EFL classroom
author: Vincent Greenier, Liu Xiao-hua, Yangyu Xiao
abstract: Abstract In Chinese primary EFL classrooms, translanguaging between English and Chinese is commonly used by teachers and students out of need for efficient communication, however, this practice has been and is still widely believed to hinder students’ English development. Although recent studies have revealed the benefits translanguaging offers for teaching and learning, little has been done to un…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在translanguaging教学实践层面直接相关，但其语境（中国小学EFL形成性评估）与你的EMI大学语法课堂存在显著差异；它更贴近你对中国教育语言政策如何塑造教师实践与感知的长期关注，而非今日稿件的核心身份协商问题。

title: Application of Artificial Intelligence powered digital writing assistant in higher education: randomized controlled trial
author: Nabi Nazari, Muhammad Salman Shabbir, Roy Setiawan
abstract: A major challenge in educational technology integration is to engage students with different affective characteristics. Also, how technology shapes attitude and learning behavior is still lacking. Findings from educational psychology and learning sciences have gained less traction in research. The present study was conducted to examine the efficacy of a group format of an Artificial Intelligence (…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"academic writing, feedback, and revision"直接相关，因为它考察AI写作辅助工具对研究生英语学术写作的干预效果，涉及反馈机制如何影响写作表现与情感态度——这与你关注的写作反馈与修改过程在主题上高度吻合。

title: Impact of ChatGPT on ESL students’ academic writing skills: a mixed methods intervention study
author: Santosh Mahapatra
abstract: Abstract This paper presents a study on the impact of ChatGPT as a formative feedback tool on the writing skills of undergraduate ESL students. Since artificial intelligence-driven automated writing evaluation tools positively impact students’ writing, ChatGPT, a generative artificial intelligence-propelled tool, can be expected to have a more substantial positive impact. However, very little empi…
relevance: 这篇论文最贴近你今日的"academic writing, feedback, and revision"扫描主线，具体切入的是AI生成反馈对ESL学生写作的干预效果，与你关注的评估如何分配权力、塑造学习实践的大框架有间接关联，但其理论深度可能停留在工具效用层面，未触及你核心关心的批判话语或语言政策维度。

title: Human-AI collaboration patterns in AI-assisted academic writing
author: Andy Nguyen, Yvonne Hong, Belle Dang et al.
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has increasingly influenced higher education, notably in academic writing where AI-powered assisting tools offer both opportunities and challenges.Recently, the rapid growth of generative AI (GAI) has brought its impacts into sharper focus, yet the dynamics of its utilisation in academic writing remain largely unexplored.This paper focuses on examining the nature of hu…
relevance: 这篇论文最贴近你今日的"academic writing, feedback, and revision"扫描主线，具体切入点是博士生与生成式AI工具协作写作时的互动策略与修改模式，与你关注的学术写作过程、反馈与修订链条直接对接，同时其"人-AI协作"框架也可延伸至你对数字平台实践中权力与能动性分配的长期关切。

title: The continuity of learning in a translanguaging science classroom
author: Annika Karlsson, Pia Nygård Larsson, Anders Jakobsson
abstract: This article aims to explore and clarify how students’ use of first and second languages in a translanguaging science classroom (TSC) may affect the continuity of learning in science. In a TSC, participants can use all available language resources, in all meaning-making situations. An ethnographic data collection and research design is used to capture the authentic language use in this practice. T…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"CSL peer interaction / negotiation of meaning / MICM"在方法-分析层高度相关：它同样以课堂互动中的语言选择为焦点，追踪学生如何在双语资源间切换以维持意义建构的连续性，这与你MICM稿件中"协商机制如何在translanguaging条件下运作"的核心问题直接对话，尤其是它对"continuity of learning"的操作化可为你模型中的机制性主张提供一个可参照的概念化路径。

title: Including multiple languages in secondary education: A translanguaging approach
author: Mirjam Günther-van der Meij, Joana Duarte, Laura Nap
abstract: Abstract This article presents recent developments around multilingual secondary education in the officially bilingual province of Friesland, the Netherlands. As in other European contexts, schools in this region face the challenge of a growing language diversity due to migration. Despite this larger variety of languages in society, schooling is still mainly through the national language (Kroon &a…
relevance: 这篇论文关注的是弗里新兰双语省份中学阶段如何通过translanguaging教学法纳入多种语言，核心议题是语言分离 vs. 语言整合的教育政策与课堂实践张力。它与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接关联较弱——你的焦点在同伴互动中的意义协商机制与语言相关片段，而这篇文章更偏向宏观的多语教育政策与translanguaging课程设计层面，更贴近你"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"和"Teacher Vulnerability / DLI"这两条稿件线。

title: Multimodality and translanguaging in negotiation of meaning
author: Laia Canals
abstract: Abstract The present study examines the role that multimodality and translanguaging play in scaffolding oral interactions during language‐related episodes (LREs) involving meaning negotiation. The oral tasks carried out using synchronous video‐based computer‐mediated communication were part of a tandem virtual exchange (Spain, Canada). The participants, 18 dyads of English and Spanish college‐leve…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, MICM）高度对齐：它直接在同步视频CMC环境下考察多模态与translanguaging如何在LRE中促进meaning negotiation，其研究设计（tandem dyads + LRE识别 + 量化与质性混合分析）与你MICM稿件的分析框架形成直接可比的方法参照。

title: “By the way I want to give you some masks”: exploring multimodal stance-taking in YouTube videos
author: Wing Yee Jenifer Ho
abstract: Abstract While the effectiveness of facemasks against COVID-19 has now become largely uncontroversial, at the beginning of the global pandemic, wearers of facemasks were often the target of sometimes racially tinged attacks. Wearing facemasks (or not) became not just a question of science, but evolved into a more complex issue of social identity, morality and global citizenship embedded within the…
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合你今日主线"社交平台多模态话语、可供性与身份建构"，且与你的Xiaohongshu稿件共享核心分析对象——双语使用者如何在视频平台上通过多模态资源（图像、言语、体态）进行立场表达(stance-taking)和身份协商。它对你最有参考价值的是方法设计层面：论文示范了如何将YouTube视频拆解为可分析的多模态数据链——把视觉元素（口罩佩戴行为）、语言选择（双语资源）、肢体表现和平台体裁特征编织成连贯的stance-taking分析序列，而不是仅将平台当作内容来源。这恰好回应你今天最需强化的写作能力，即如何把图片、视频、文字等平台要素写成系统性的数据链。不过需要注意，该文聚焦的是YouTube视频文本本身的多模态立场分析，与你稿件中强调的评论区互动、translanguaging的不对称功能以及"数字第三空间"的理论定位存在差异，因此它更适合作为方法操作化层面的参照，而非理论框架或问题意识的直接借鉴。

title: Cross-platform language learning: A spatial perspective on narratives of language learning across digital platforms
author: Yeong-Ju Lee, Peter Roger
abstract: In the current reality of digital activity, individuals often explore more than one digital platform for language learning. Despite increasing research on the uses of digital platforms for language learning in informal contexts, the cross-platform experience remains in need of elucidation. Drawing on spatial perspectives on digital technology and language learning to conceptualize digital platform…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"社交平台多模态话语、可供性与身份建构"高度契合，尤其在"将数字平台概念化为空间而非背景"这一理论层面直接回应了你当前写作中最需要强化的问题——它用spatial perspective把跨平台经验从"使用描述"提升为"空间实践分析"，这对你在Xiaohongshu稿件中论证"数字第三空间"的理论定位极具参照价值。

title: Teachers as influencers and personal brands on Instagram: double-tapping language ideologies in the attention economy
author: Ali Fuad Selvi
abstract: Abstract Informed by the platformization and (micro)celebrification of language teachers as social media influencers, this study examines the underexplored nexus of influencer culture, language ideology, and entrepreneurial self-branding in ELT. It analyzes the self-positioning of 48 Instagram edu-influencers creating English language content for Turkish-speaking audiences. Using the Discourse His…
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日主线"社交平台多模态话语、可供性与身份建构"，且与Beyond Deficit/Xiaohongshu稿件高度同构：两者都将社交平台上的语言教育内容生产者作为分析对象，都关注平台可供性（Instagram metadata vs. 小红书图文/评论区）如何塑造语言意识形态与自我品牌化的身份展演，都处于批判话语研究与数字平台实践的交叉地带。

title: Diaspora micro-influencers and COVID-19 communication on social media: The case of Chinese-speaking YouTube vloggers
author: Leticia-Tian Zhang, Sumin Zhao
abstract: Abstract Diaspora vloggers–migrants who produce video blogs in the language of their home countries for a transnational diaspora community–have been a largely overlooked group in the studies of social media. This paper focuses on the unique role of Chinese diaspora vloggers during an unprecedented global event—the COVID-19 pandemic. Using manual keyword search (e.g., zhaijia riji , faguo yiqing ) …
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Chinese-English translanguaging in digital third spaces and identity work"在平台语境与身份建构层面相关，但关联有限：它聚焦的是华人diaspora vloggers在YouTube上通过单一语言（中文）内容进行跨国信息传播与身份协商，核心议题是健康传播与micro-influencer角色，而非translanguaging的功能分类或多语实践本身。因此它更多属于"共享研究场景（diaspora + social media + identity）"的部分相关，而非理论或方法层的直接对话对象。

title: Digital multimodal composing in L2 classrooms: A research agenda
author: Lianjiang Jiang, Christoph A. Hafner
abstract: Abstract Research on digital multimodal composing (DMC) in second language (L2) classrooms has proliferated considerably in recent years, to a large extent in response to the changing digital and multimodal communication landscape. This article offers a research agenda on DMC in L2 classrooms. We begin with a theoretically oriented overview of DMC scholarship. We then examine seven research themes…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（数字第三空间中的translanguaging与身份建构）处于"部分相关——领域邻近但焦点不同"的层面：它聚焦的是L2课堂中数字多模态写作（DMC）的研究议程，虽然涉及多模态资源与数字环境，但核心关切是课堂教学中的composing过程与任务设计，而非用户自发的平台translanguaging实践及其身份功能，因此理论对话对象和数据场景都与你的Xiaohongshu稿件存在明显距离。

title: Fan translation of games, anime, and fanfiction
author: Boris Vázquez-Calvo, Leticia-Tian Zhang, Mariona Pascual et al.
abstract: Fan practices involving translation open up opportunities to explore language learning practices within the fandom (Sauro, 2017). relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"digital third spaces中的translanguaging与identity work"在平台语境下的非正式语言实践与身份建构这一层相关，但贴合度有限：它聚焦的是粉丝翻译（fan translation）作为语言学习的途径，而非translanguaging的功能分类或权力/deficit话语的批判，研究场域（游戏、动漫、同人文）与你的小红书多模态数字第三空间也存在距离。

title: The Perfect Storm: A Developmental–Sociocultural Framework for the Role of Social Media in Adolescent Girls’ Body Image Concerns and Mental Health
author: Sophia Choukas‐Bradley, Savannah R. Roberts, Anne J. Maheux et al.
journal: Clinical Child and Family Psychology Review
relevance: 这篇论文聚焦社交媒体对青少年女孩身体意象与心理健康的影响，提出"发展-社会文化"整合框架，与你的研究主线（classroom interaction, mediation, participation）几乎没有直接交集；它与你活跃稿件中最接近的"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"仅在"社交平台话语如何塑造身份"这一层有微弱的主题关联，但其关注对象（青少年心理健康）、理论根基（发展心理学×社会文化心理学）和数据类型都与你的批判话语/多模态分析取向不同。

title: Thinking through other minds: A variational approach to cognition and culture
author: Samuel P. L. Veissière, Axel Constant, Maxwell J. D. Ramstead et al.
abstract: The processes underwriting the acquisition of culture remain unclear. How are shared habits, norms, and expectations learned and maintained with precision and reliability across large-scale sociocultural ensembles? Is there a unifying account of the mechanisms involved in the acquisition of culture? Notions such as "shared expectations," the "selective patterning of attention and behaviour," "cult…
relevance: 这篇论文探讨文化习得的认知机制，试图用变分推理（variational inference）统一解释共享期望、注意力模式化与隐性学习等概念，与你今日主线"课堂互动、中介与参与"的直接关联较弱——它处于认知科学与文化理论的元层面，而非课堂交互数据的实证分析层面。

title: Rethinking the entwinement between artificial intelligence and human learning: What capabilities do learners need for a world with AI?
author: Lina Markauskaitė, Rebecca Marrone, Oleksandra Poquet et al.
abstract: The proliferation of AI in many aspects of human life—from personal leisure, to collaborative professional work, to global policy decisions—poses a sharp question about how to prepare people for an interconnected, fast-changing world which is increasingly becoming saturated with technological devices and agentic machines. What kinds of capabilities do people need in a world infused with AI? How ca…
relevance: 这篇论文讨论AI时代学习者所需能力及其对话性概念化，属于宏观教育哲学层面的思辨，与你今日主线"classroom interaction, mediation, and participation"仅在最宽泛的"学习者能力发展"层面有交集，并不直接涉及课堂互动数据的分析或参与机制的实证呈现。它对你活跃稿件中任何一篇的推进作用都相当有限。

title: Teacher professional identities and their impacts on translanguaging pedagogies in a STEM EMI classroom context in China: a nexus analysis
author: Amy Wanyu Ou, Mingyue Gu
abstract: While translanguaging has gained increasing recognition as a multiliteracy pedagogy in English-medium instruction (EMI) education, research exploring its implementation in STEM classroom contexts remains limited. Furthermore, the interplay of EMI teachers’ professional identities and their instructional strategies has received little attention. This qualitative study explores how STEM academics in…
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"，且同样聚焦中国EMI课堂中教师身份与translanguaging实践的交互关系——区别在于它考察的是STEM学科教师而非语言/语法课堂，且采用nexus analysis而非action research，因此可以作为你稿件在"研究场景"和"方法论取径"上的直接对话对象与差异化定位参照。

title: Taking communication to task once more – a further decade on
author: Martin East
abstract: Task-based language teaching (TBLT) emerged in the 1980s as a learner-centred and experiential alternative to more established and teacher-led approaches to acquiring an additional language (L2). relevance: 这篇论文是对任务型语言教学（TBLT）长达三十年发展的综述性回顾，其核心关切在于TBLT作为教学法框架的理论演变与实施争议。与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"的关联仅停留在宏观教学法讨论层面：TBLT文献中关于学习者中心、交际真实性与课堂实施张力的讨论，可以作为你论证EMI课堂中translanguaging为何需要超越既有教学法框架的背景铺垫，但它并未直接触及translanguaging、身份协商或行动研究中的任何一个核心概念。

title: Funds of knowledge for synchronous online language teaching: a translanguaging view on an ESL teacher’s pedagogical practices
author: Kevin W. H. Tai

abstract: Abstract In recent years, there has been a significant increase in the integration of synchronous online teaching tools in language teaching. relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论层高度相关：它将translanguaging与funds of knowledge框架结合，用于分析教师如何在实际教学中调动多元知识资源——这恰好可以帮助你在EMI稿件中论证translanguaging不仅是语言混用现象，更是教师有意识地连接语言资源与社会文化知识的教学实践，从而为你的"transformative practice"论点提供理论对话点。

title: Virtual communities as breathing spaces for minority languages: Re-framing minority language use in social media

relevance: 这篇论文聚焦少数语言在社交媒体虚拟社区中的活力维持与translanguaging实践，最贴近你"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"这条研究主线——即数字平台上的多语实践与身份建构——而非今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）。它对你今天正在推进的机制性写作（如何让MICM模型的主张可检验、语气可控）帮助有限。

title: Democratization of the Classroom through Teachers’ Language Choices and Practices in Multilingual Classrooms of Zambia
author: Vigirio Bwalya, C Baker, F Banda et al.

abstract: Considering the argument that Zambia is a multilingual country and that all the classrooms are multilingual while the current Language in Education Policy only mentions English as the language of instruction from grade five onwards, it was imperative to study the sociolinguistic situation of the grade 6 classrooms of Chibombo district and their corresponding teachers' language practices. relevance: 这篇论文聚焦赞比亚多语课堂中教师的语言选择与权力分配，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接关联较弱——它关注的是教育语言政策层面的课堂民主化，而非学习者之间的意义协商或语言相关片段的微观互动机制。如果硬要对接，它更贴近你长期画像中"语言政策如何在课堂互动中分配权力"这条线，以及 DLI/EMI 相关稿件，而非今天的 MICM 稿件。

title: Translanguaging in education
author: Florence Bonacina-Pugh, Ildegrada da Costa Cabral, Jing Huang
abstract: Abstract This state-of-the-art review focuses on translanguaging in education. In recent years, scholars have engaged in the conceptualisation of ‘translanguaging’ (e.g. García, 2009; García & Wei, 2014a; Wei, 2018) as well as in conducting a vast and ever-increasing number of empirical studies, in educational contexts in particular. This article aims to take stock of the different ways in whi…
relevance: 这篇综述系统梳理了translanguaging在教育场景中的概念化路径与实证应用，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的关联主要在理论定位层：你的MICM稿件需要明确说明translanguaging视角如何为peer interaction中的语言相关片段（LRE）和意义协商提供解释框架，而这篇综述恰好能帮你快速定位translanguaging在课堂互动研究中已被验证的边界与争议点，从而让你在引用该概念时更精准地控制其解释范围，避免概念过度延伸。

title: Linking Learners’ Perspectives on Language Assessment Practices to Teachers’ Assessment Literacy Enhancement (TALE): Insights from Four European Countries

relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"language assessment, fairness, and validity"直接相关，但切入角度偏向assessment literacy与formative assessment实践的跨国需求分析，而非深入探讨construct定义或fairness/validity的理论论证——因此它属于"主题层相关但理论深度有限"的一类。

title: English language assessment in Bangladesh today: principles, practices, and problems
author: Md Shaiful Islam, Md. Kamrul Hasan, Shahin Sultana et al.

relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"language assessment, fairness, and validity"直接相关，但它属于国别语言评估政策的宏观描述与批判，侧重孟加拉国课程改革与考试实践之间的落差（washback / policy-practice gap），与你AP Chinese / LAQ稿件中关注的construct alignment和fairness框架有主题层面的呼应，但理论深度和分析粒度差异较大。

title: Embodied learning and teaching approaches in language education: A mixed studies review
author: Sofia Jusslin, Kaisa Korpinen, Niina Lilja et al.
abstract: The notion of embodied learning has gained ground in educational sciences over the last decade and has made its way to language education with researchers acknowledging language learning as an embodied process. This mixed studies review aggregates and reviews empirical research, published from 1990 to 2020, using embodied learning approaches in language education. The review focuses on embodied ap…
relevance: 这篇论文关注的是具身学习（embodied learning）在语言教育中的应用，属于教学法与认知取向的综述研究，与你今日主线——数字第三空间中的中英translanguaging与身份建构——在理论层和研究对象上距离较远，仅在"语言学习中多模态资源的作用"这一宽泛层面有微弱交集。

title: Mobile communication and refugees: An analytical review of academic literature
author: Amanda Aléncar
abstract: Abstract Over the past decade, an expanding literature has explored the ways in which refugees rely on mobile communication technologies to stay in touch with a wider community and to access relevant information and services in their new places. Nevertheless, challenges linked to (a lack of) digital literacy and accessibility among refugees, and associated risks of technology use can negatively af…
relevance: 这篇论文关注难民如何利用移动通信技术维系社群联系、获取资源并协商参与身份，其"数字平台作为弱势群体赋权/去权空间"的分析框架与你在Xiaohongshu稿件中将数字第三空间概念化为不对称权力协商场所的思路存在结构性对应，但它聚焦的是难民与移动技术的关系，而非translanguaging的语言功能分类，因此只在"平台语境如何形塑身份实践"这一层面上部分相关。

title: Critical Language Testing, Multilingualism and Social Justice
author: Elana Shohamy
abstract: Abstract The paper reports on trends in language testing taking place over the years and aim at critical perspectives of testing and promoting inclusion, equity and justice. It begins with critical theories by Messick, Foucault and Bourdieu, leading to critical language testing (CLT) which focused on consequences and uses of tests. Given the power of tests and their detrimental effects questions w…
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, and epistemic authority"的制度批判层：它从Messick效度后果论、Foucault权力分析和Bourdieu资本理论出发，搭建"critical language testing (CLT)"框架，审视标准化语言测试如何对双/多语学生制造系统性不公——这与你LAQ稿件中论证AP Chinese考试如何通过评分标准与文体规范制度性地分配认识权威（epistemic authority）、造成元语言不对称的核心问题意识高度吻合。

title: The effect of collaborative writing in an EFL secondary setting
author: Izaskun Villarreal, Nora Gil-Sarratea
abstract: Collaborative writing (CW) has been proven advantageous to enhance the second and foreign language skills of university students. However, little research to date has explored whether CW practices are fruitful for secondary school learners in foreign language (FL) contexts, a population characterized by low language proficiency levels, and few opportunities to engage with the FL. The present class…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（AP Chinese assessment, metalinguistic asymmetry, epistemic authority）几乎没有直接关联：它关注的是EFL中学生协作写作中的语言相关片段（LREs），既不涉及评估文件的制度性分析，也不触及元语言不对称或认识权威的问题。如果硬要找连接点，它所处理的"低输入环境下学习者的元语言互动"在最宽泛的层面上可与你对FL课堂互动的兴趣（MICM/CSL Interaction线）挂钩，但与今天需要强化的制度性论证和文件证据分析毫无方法论借鉴价值。

title: A review of the benefits and drawbacks of high-stakes final examinations in higher education
author: Sarah French, Ashton L. Dickerson, Raoul A. Mulder
abstract: Abstract High-stakes examinations enjoy widespread use as summative assessments in higher education. We review the arguments for and against their use, across seven common themes: memory recall and knowledge retention; student motivation and learning; authenticity and real-world relevance; validity and reliability; academic misconduct and contract cheating; stress, anxiety and wellbeing; and fairn…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"AP Chinese assessment"在评估制度批判层面有表面交集，但其关注的是高等教育中高风险终结性考试的一般性利弊综述，既不涉及语言评估的特殊性（如元语言不对称、认识论权威），也不触及文件层面的话语/政策分析，因此与你的具体论证距离较远。

title: Sticky Objects and Places: Entangled Emotions in an English‐Medium University Educationscape
author: Sarah Hopkyns
abstract: ABSTRACT This paper explores entangled emotions in an Abu Dhabi English‐medium university educationscape in relation to sticky objects and sticky places, which are objects/places saturated in affect through familiarity and repetition. Moving away from a lingua‐centric approach to linguistic landscape research, this ethnographic study explores Emirati students’ emotions not only in relation to lang…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在EMI情境与身份协商层面有交集，但其核心关注点是情感地理学与符号景观（linguistic/semiotic landscape），而非课堂教学实践或translanguaging pedagogy，因此属于主题部分相关、理论视角可参考的层次。

title: Understanding intercultural virtual exchange through a translanguaging lens in Chinese as a foreign language
author: Zhiyan Guo, Xinyue Xu
journal: Journal of China Computer-Assisted Language Learning
abstract: Abstract Virtual exchange refers to technology-enabled online communication between people who are geographically separated from each other. It has been increasingly adopted in education in the past two decades, especially since early 2020 when teachers and students were forced to move to an online mode of teaching and international exchange owing to the most recent pandemic. The current study is …
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论视角上直接相关——它同样以translanguaging lens审视课堂实践，但语境是跨文化虚拟交流中的CFL学习者，而非你所聚焦的EMI语法课堂。两者共享"translanguaging如何调节身份协商"这一核心问题，但该文的远程交际情境与你的面对面行动研究在方法论和数据类型上差异较大，因此相关性主要停留在理论框架层面，而非方法可移植性。

title: Enacting Colonial and Neoliberal Governmentalities through American ELT Programs in Pakistan
author: Waqar Ali Shah
abstract: Abstract Several scholars have examined the increasing influence of neoliberalism in TESOL/ELT, including pedagogy, curricula, and policy discourses. These neoliberal rationalities are, however, not detached from coloniality in Global Southern countries. Taking Pakistan as a case study, the present study examines how neoliberal subjectivities intersect with colonial logics in US‐funded ELT program…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（EMI课堂中的translanguaging与身份协商）仅在宏观批判话语层面有间接关联——它处理的是ELT项目如何承载殖民性与新自由主义治理逻辑，而你的稿件聚焦的是课堂微观互动中translanguaging如何促成身份转变，二者的分析尺度和经验场域差距较大。

title: Humorous Translanguaging and Playful Discourse on Indonesian Twitter: A Critical Discourse Analysis
author: Dwi Indarti, Luciana Luciana
abstract: Humor and translanguaging are increasingly prominent in digital communication; however, limited research has explored their intersection in Indonesian social media discourse, where local and global languages creatively merge. This gap leaves unanswered questions about how humorous and multilingual practices operate in online spaces, especially in contexts of cultural hybridity and diverse language…
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日主线中"digital discourse + translanguaging"的交叉点，且平台语境（Indonesian Twitter/X）与你"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"稿件在研究对象层面形成镜像——同样关注社交平台上多语实践如何建构身份与权力关系，只是语言生态不同。最值得你批判性借鉴的恰恰是它可能暴露的问题而非优点：它采用Fairclough三维模型做CDA，但从摘要看，"humor""translanguaging""cultural hybridity"三个大概念并置，研究问题的锐度不足，容易滑向你今天想避免的"概念堆叠"写法——观察它如何（未能）把理论框架压缩为服务具体分析论证的工具，可以反向帮你在小红书稿件中把CDA或translanguaging框架写得更节制、更有方法论纪律，而不是让框架本身变成装饰。

title: “Multilingual islands in the monolingual sea”: Foreign languages in the South Korean linguistic landscape
author: Kapitolina Fedorova, Hye Hyun Nam
abstract: Abstract The article aims at analyzing data on the South Korean linguistic landscape, with a focus on multilingual practices and different dimensions of language use, sets of norms, and ideological constructs underling particular linguistic choices. It is based on the analysis of a data set of over 800 digital photos of various signs and advertisements as well as necessary metadata gathered in 201…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"digital discourse and multilingual practice"中的多语实践维度直接相关，但它关注的是物理空间的语言景观（linguistic landscape）而非数字平台话语，因此与你活跃稿件中的Xiaohongshu多模态话语或EMI translanguaging的契合度停留在方法启发层面，而非核心理论对话层。

title: Digital polycentricity and diasporic connectivity: A Norwegian‐Senegalese case study
author: Jannis Androutsopoulos, Kristin Vold Lexander
abstract: Abstract Digital communication remains largely unexplored in sociolinguistic research on diaspora and language. In this paper, ethnographically collected data from Norway‐based families of Senegalese heritage are explored to identify how family members use digital media to engage with diaspora concerns and projects, and how this engagement shapes their multilingual practices online. We re‐contextu…
relevance: 这篇论文最贴近你今日主线中"digital discourse + multilingual practice"的交叉点，同时与Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu稿件在方法论层面形成对话——两者都涉及如何在数字平台上追踪多语实践而不把平台本身写成铁板一块的单一空间。论文将polycentricity从物理邻里重新语境化到数字生态，提出四个"centres"的分析框架，这种做法示范了如何把一个既有理论概念做精准的场域迁移，而非简单嫁接或堆叠。

title: Implementing translanguaging pedagogies in an English medium instruction course
author: Beñat Muguruza, Jasone Cenoz, Durk Gorter
abstract: English medium education at university level is widely used in different contexts and it poses a challenge for students who are not used to studying through the medium of English. This study was carried out in a university in the Basque Country in Spain and it focuses on a course where students who are mostly Basque–Spanish bilinguals are taught through the medium of English. The course teacher ap…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）关联较弱，但与你的活跃稿件"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"直接对口：它考察的是EMI课堂中教师如何实施灵活语言政策、学生如何回应多语实践，这正是你那篇行动研究稿件所处的经验场景。

title: Examining Pedagogical Translanguaging: A Systematic Review of the Literature
author: Marina Prilutskaya
abstract: In the past two decades translanguaging has proven to be a potent concept in applied linguistics, having generated a large amount of literature that explores theoretical and empirical dimensions of this linguistically inclusive pedagogical approach to language teaching and learning. This systematic literature review focuses on empirical studies that draw on the translanguaging framework in English…
relevance: 这篇系统综述聚焦translanguaging pedagogy的实证研究全景，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接对接点有限，但与活跃稿件中的"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"高度相关，也间接关联"MICM"稿件中可能涉及的translanguaging作为peer interaction中意义协商资源的理论定位。

title: Diversity and Standards in Writing for Publication in the Age of AI—Between a Rock and a Hard Place
author: Maria Kuteeva, Marta Andersson
abstract: Abstract Research communities across disciplines recognize the need to diversify and decolonize knowledge. While artificial intelligence-supported large language models (LLMs) can help with access to knowledge generated in the Global North and demystify publication practices, they are still biased toward dominant norms and knowledge paradigms. LLMs lack agency, metacognition, knowledge of the loca…
relevance: 这篇论文讨论AI大语言模型对学术写作多样性与标准化之间张力的影响，属于学术写作与知识生产政治的宏观议题，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, MICM）没有直接关联，也不触及课堂互动微观机制或编码方法层面的问题。它与你的长期写作目标中"如何表达贡献与局限"有间接呼应，但距离你当前需要强化的"把机制性主张写得可检验、控制exploratory语气"这一具体写作能力较远。

title: Mobile Augmented Reality and Language‐Related Episodes
author: Tetyana Sydorenko, John Hellermann, Steven L. Thorne et al.
abstract: Applications of locative media (e.g., place‐based mobile augmented reality [ AR ]) are used in various educational content areas and have been shown to provide learners with valuable opportunities for investigation‐based learning, location‐situated social and collaborative interaction, and embodied experience of place (Squire, 2009; Thorne & Hellermann, 2017; Zheng et al., 2018). Mobile locati…
relevance: 这篇论文关注移动增强现实（AR）环境中的语言相关片段（LREs），属于技术辅助语言学习（TALL）与互动研究的交叉领域。它与你今日主线"数字第三空间中的translanguaging与身份建构"仅在"数字/技术中介的语言实践"这一宽泛层面相关，但在核心关切上差异明显：该研究聚焦的是SLA互动框架下的LRE构念与任务设计，而非translanguaging的功能分类、多模态符号资源或身份论证。

title: Distributed agency in second language learning and teaching through generative AI
author: Robert Godwin‐Jones
abstract: Generative AI offers significant opportunities for language learning. Tools like ChatGPT provide second language practice through chats in written or voice formats, with the learner specifying through prompts conversational parameters. AI can be instructed to give corrective feedback and create practice exercises. Using AI, instructors can build learning and assessment materials in a variety of me…
relevance: 这篇论文讨论生成式AI在二语学习与教学中的"distributed agency"，核心关切在于技术如何重新分配学习者与教师的能动性——这与你今日主线（Xiaohongshu上的translanguaging与身份建构）在"数字空间中的agency分配"层面有概念上的间接关联，但它聚焦的是AI工具而非社交平台的多模态话语实践，既不涉及translanguaging的功能分类，也不处理平台affordance如何塑造身份，因此只是边缘相关。

title: Translanguaging and embodied teaching and learning: lessons from a multilingual karate club in London
author: Zhu Hua, Li Wei, Daria Jankowicz-Pytel
journal: International Journal of Bilingual Education and Bilingualism
abstract: The purpose of this article is to explore the role of embodied repertories in teaching and learning in a multi-ethnic karate club in East London and its implications for language teaching and learning. We do so through the lens of translanguaging and apply the concept of translanguaging space where diverse semiotic systems are integrated and orchestrated. Through a close examination of how teachin…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"digital third spaces中的translanguaging与identity work"共享translanguaging space这一核心理论透镜，但其语境（线下武术俱乐部的具身互动）与你的数字平台多模态分析差异显著，属于理论框架层的间接相关而非直接对话对象。

title: What can interactional sociolinguistics bring to the family language policy research table? The case of a Malay family in Singapore
author: Seyed Hadi Mirvahedi
journal: Journal of Multilingual and Multicultural Development
abstract: This research applies interactional sociolinguistics within a family language policy framework to investigate how social structures and institutional discourses outside the home trickle into daily mundane activities within a Malay-English bilingual family in Singapore. Drawing upon ethnographic interviews and naturally-occurring interactions at home, the study examines the parents’ reported lingui…
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日"language policy, ideology, and critical discourse"的扫描主线，尤其与你关注的制度话语如何渗透微观实践（如家庭互动中的语言混用与意识形态协商）高度相关，也可为你"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"和"Teacher Vulnerability / DLI"稿件中"宏观制度压力如何在日常话语层被感知与再生产"这一论证逻辑提供参照案例。

title: Pride, prejudice and pragmatism: family language policies in the UK
author: Xiao Lan Curdt‐Christiansen, Li Wei, Zhu Hua
abstract: Abstract In this study, we examine how mobility and on-going changes in sociocultural contexts impact family language policy (FLP) in the UK. Using a questionnaire and involving 470 transnational families across the UK, our study provides a descriptive analysis of different family language practices in England and establishes how attitudes influence the different types of FLP in these families. Co…
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日"language policy, ideology, and critical discourse"的广扫主线，尤其与你关注的语言意识形态如何在制度与日常层面分配权力、塑造身份的核心问题高度相关——它考察的是英国跨国家庭如何在流动性与社会文化变迁中协商语言实践，态度与意识形态如何驱动不同类型的家庭语言政策，这与你在DLI教师处境、小红书多语实践等稿件中反复触及的"意识形态—实践—身份"链条形成直接对话。

title: English medium instruction in South Asia’s multilingual schools: unpacking the dynamics of ideological orientations, policy/practices, and democratic questions
author: Pramod K. Sah
journal: International Journal of Bilingual Education and Bilingualism
abstract: This article provides a critical review of English medium instruction (EMI) policy/practices in the K-12 multilingual schools in South Asia, especially in Nepal, India, and Pakistan. Employing Bourdieu’s (1993) lens of ‘linguistic capital’ and ‘linguistic marketplace,’ the article takes stock of (a) the development of EMI and its ideological and pedagogic motivations, (b) the models of EMI policy …
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日"language policy, ideology, and critical discourse"的扫描主线，同时与你的EMI Translanguaging / Action Research稿件高度相关——它从南亚K-12情境审视EMI政策如何在多语社会中再生产语言等级与不平等，与你关注的EMI课堂转语实践共享"制度如何分配语言权力"这一核心问题意识。

title: Translanguaging in Sweden: A critical review from an international perspective
author: Carles Fuster, Camilla Bardel
abstract: ‘Translanguaging’ is nowadays an umbrella term referring to either the framework of ‘spontaneous translanguaging’ from the United States or that of ‘pedagogical translanguaging’ from the Basque Country. Both these frameworks propose ways of integrating languages in school, but they adopt very different perspectives on multilingualism, language education and minority languages. This article starts …
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在理论框架辨析层高度相关。它区分了"spontaneous translanguaging"（美国脉络）与"pedagogical translanguaging"（巴斯克脉络）两大框架在多语观、语言教育目标及少数语言立场上的根本分歧，这正是你EMI稿件中必须做出的理论定位选择——你的中国EMI语法课堂里的translanguaging实践究竟更靠近哪一端，或如何在两者之间建立自己的分析立场，直接决定论证的尖锐度。

title: ESL classroom interactions in a translanguaging space
author: Sarah Hui-Ching Lin, Alex Ho‐Cheong Leung
abstract: Abstract There has been a recent proliferation of studies pertaining to translanguaging. This impetus is largely driven by the increasing acknowledgement of daily communications as translingual practice. In fact, the closely related construct of plurilingualism has been incorporated into the development of the companion volume of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (Council of…
relevance: 这篇论文直接贴合你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"，因为它聚焦ESL课堂互动中的translanguaging空间，与你稿件中对EMI语法课堂translanguaging实践的分析在理论构念和经验场景上高度重叠，尤其是如何将课堂层面的translanguaging互动数据转化为研究发现。

title: ‘It becomes increasingly complex to deal with multiple channels’: materialised communicative competence and digital inequality in English-medium higher education in the digital era
author: Amy Wanyu Ou, Hans Malmström
journal: Journal of Multilingual and Multicultural Development
abstract: This article explores university students’ communicative competence for English-Medium Instruction (EMI) at a Swedish university in the era of digitalisation and blended learning. Based on a linguistic ethnography, we present an argument for communicative competence as repertoire assemblages orchestrating digital materiality and human language to construct meanings. The study shows how diverse dig…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Translanguaging, identity negotiation, and action research in EMI classrooms"在EMI语境层高度相关，但切入角度不同：它聚焦的是数字物质性（digital materiality）与交际能力的重新概念化，而非translanguaging作为课堂转化实践或身份协商的过程。它更贴近你"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"那条关注数字平台多模态话语的主线，而非当前EMI行动研究稿件的核心论证。

title: Understanding and shaping the future of work with self-determination theory
author: Marylène Gagné, Sharon K. Parker, Mark Griffin et al.
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线"Teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, and institutional discourse"有直接的理论框架层关联。自我决定理论（SDT）中的自主、胜任和归属三个基本心理需求，恰好可以作为你分析DLI教师在制度话语中"脆弱性"来源的理论透镜——教师感到vulnerable，往往正是因为制度环境系统性地压缩了其自主空间、否定其专业胜任感或切断其归属联结。

title: “Extending the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) to Predict University Students’ Intentions to Use Metaverse-Based Learning Platforms”
author: Ahmad Samed Al‐Adwan, Na Li, Amer Al-Adwan et al.
abstract: Metaverse, which combines a number of information technologies, is the Internet of the future. A media for immersive learning, metaverse could set future educational trends and lead to significant reform in education. Although the metaverse has the potential to improve the effectiveness of online learning experiences, metaverse-based educational implementations are still in their infancy. Addition…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, institutional discourse）以及活跃稿件均无实质关联。它聚焦于技术接受模型在元宇宙学习平台中的量化扩展，属于教育技术采纳研究，既不涉及语言政策、制度话语或教师处境，也不涉及批判话语分析或质性方法论。

title: A Metaverse: Taxonomy, Components, Applications, and Open Challenges
author: Sangmin Park, Young‐Gab Kim
abstract: Unlike previous studies on the Metaverse based on Second Life, the current Metaverse is based on the social value of Generation Z that online and offline selves are not different. With the technological development of deep learning-based high-precision recognition models and natural generation models, Metaverse is being strengthened with various factors, from mobile-based always-on access to conne…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日的研究主线（teacher vulnerability, dual language immersion, institutional discourse）以及更广泛的应用语言学与批判话语研究几乎没有实质关联。它聚焦于元宇宙的技术分类体系（深度学习模型、虚拟货币、数字孪生等），既不涉及语言教育政策、教师身份与制度话语，也不提供可迁移的理论框架或方法论资源。唯一可能的远端联系是其对"线上与线下自我不可分割"这一Generation Z社会价值观的提及，但该论文并未从话语分析或身份建构角度展开，因此对你当前需要强化的"把个人经历转化为结构分析"的写作能力也无借鉴价值。

title: Sustaining Critical Approaches to Translanguaging in Education: A Contextual Framework
author: Anna Mendoza, Laura Hamman‐Ortiz, Zhongfeng Tian et al.
abstract: Abstract Translanguaging remains a timely and important topic in bi/multilingual education. The most recent turn in translanguaging scholarship involves attention to translanguaging in context in response to critiques of translanguaging as a universally empowering educational practice. In this paper, seven early career translanguaging scholars propose a framework for researching translanguaging “i…
relevance: 这篇论文与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction / MICM）的直接关联度有限，但与你活跃稿件中"EMI Translanguaging / Action Research"及"Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu"更为贴近——它提出的"在具体情境中审视translanguaging"的分层框架（借用Douglas Fir Group的宏观-中观-微观层次）恰好回应了你在MICM稿件中需要处理的一个侧面问题：如何把课堂互动中的微观机制（如LRE、negotiation of meaning）锚定在更宽的制度与意识形态语境中，而不让分析停留在去语境化的编码计数上。

title: Digital literacies and language learning
author: Rodney H. Jones
abstract: This chapter focuses both on how people’s practices of using digital technologies affect the ways they use and learn language, and how people’s practices of using and learning language affect their use of digital technologies. Over the past two decades, researchers interested in digital literacies and language learning have focused on how people’s communicative practices in digital environments ch…
relevance: 这篇论文属于数字素养与语言学习的宏观综述章节，与你今日主线（CSL peer interaction, negotiation of meaning, LRE, MICM）仅在"技术中介的交际实践如何影响语言学习"这一层面有间接关联，并不直接触及课堂同伴互动的微观机制建模或编码方案设计，因此相关性停留在背景层而非核心层。

title: Negotiating the Multilingual Turn in SLA
author: Stephen May
abstract: To everything (Turn, Turn, Turn) There is a season (Turn, Turn, Turn) … The Byrds IN 2011, THE PROMINENT EUROPEAN political theorist, Van Parijs, published Linguistic justice for Europe and for the world (Van Parijs, 2011). relevance: 这篇论文最贴近你今日的广扫主线（sociolinguistics, bilingualism, and identity），但它的切入点偏向SLA领域内部的"多语转向"元理论讨论——与你活跃稿件中直接处理课堂互动、教师处境或平台话语的经验研究距离较远，属于概念层相关而非方法或数据层相关。

title: Rethinking multilingual experience through a Systems Framework of Bilingualism
author: Debra Titone, Mehrgol Tiv
abstract: Abstract In “The Devil's Dictionary”, Bierce (1911) defined language as “The music with which we charm the serpents guarding another's treasure.” This satirical definition reflects a core truth – humans communicate using language to accomplish social goals. In this Keynote, we urge cognitive scientists and neuroscientists to more fully embrace sociolinguistic and sociocultural experiences as part …
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日"sociolinguistics, bilingualism, and identity"的广扫主线，且与你多篇活跃稿件（EMI Translanguaging、Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu、MICM / CSL Interaction）共享一个核心张力：如何把双语/多语经验从认知-神经科学的个体化框架拉回到社会文化与社会语言学的视野中。Systems Framework of Bilingualism 试图在认知与社会之间搭桥，这与你在多篇稿件中需要处理的"个体语言实践 ↔ 制度/社会结构"之间的理论衔接问题高度同构。

title: Bringing Race Into Second Language Acquisition
author: Nelson Flores, Jonathan Rosa
abstract: RECENTLY, SEVERAL NEWS ARTICLES WERE published celebrating the bilingualism of Princess Charlotte, the young daughter of Prince William and Catherine Middleton. A headline from Independent noted, “Princess Charlotte is already bilingual at age two,” with the author insisting that this was “a skill most people cannot claim” and that she “can't help but feel inferior” (Ritschel, 2018). In a similar …
relevance: 这篇论文直接对应你今日主线"sociolinguistics, bilingualism, and identity"中最具批判性的一环：种族（race）作为被系统性忽略的变量如何塑造了"双语能力"在公共话语与学术话语中的价值分配。它与你活跃稿件中的 Beyond Deficit / Xiaohongshu 和 Teacher Vulnerability / DLI 两条线高度相关——前者涉及平台话语中对多语者的赤字叙事解构，后者涉及双语教育制度中教师身份的权力处境，而这篇文章恰恰为"谁的双语被赞美、谁的双语被病理化"提供了种族化视角的理论锚点。

title: Global English and Political Economy
author: John P. O’Regan
abstract: In this book, John O’Regan examines the role of political economy in the worldwide spread of English and traces the origins and development of the dominance of English to the endless accumulation of capital in a capitalist world-system. O’Regan combines Marxist perspectives of capital accumulation with world-systems analysis, international political economy, and studies of imperialism and empire t…
relevance: 这篇专著从马克思主义政治经济学与世界体系分析的视角解释英语全球扩散，与你今日主线（小红书上中英translanguaging的功能分类与身份建构）仅在宏观批判层面有间接关联——它提供了"英语霸权如何通过资本网络再生产"的结构性背景，但并不直接触及数字平台实践、多模态话语或translanguaging的微观功能分析。

title: Examining the Relationship Between Grit and Foreign Language Performance: Enjoyment and Anxiety as Mediators
author: Eerdemutu Liu, Junju Wang
abstract: The relationship between grit and success has been investigated extensively in various contexts. However, the association between grit and language performance, especially in a Chinese high school context, remains underexplored.
